insight.headline: Storefront API Update
insight.what_changed: The Storefront API now supports the @inContext directive with an optional channelId argument, allowing developers to apply a specific sales channel's context to an entire query. This change is available in Storefront API version 2026-10. The @inContext directive enables developers to fetch data specific to a particular sales channel, such as online store or point of sale.
insight.developer_impact: Developers should update their Storefront API queries to utilize the new @inContext directive with the channelId argument. This will enable them to fetch data specific to a particular sales channel, providing a more accurate and relevant experience for merchants and customers. When using the @inContext directive, developers should ensure they are handling errors and exceptions properly, as the API may return different data or error messages based on the sales channel context. Additionally, developers should review the Storefront API documentation to understand the available channels and their corresponding IDs.
insight.merchant_impact: Merchants will benefit from more accurate and relevant data being displayed in their sales channels, such as online store or point of sale, resulting in a better overall customer experience.
insight.why_it_matters: This update provides developers with more flexibility and control over the data they fetch from the Storefront API, enabling them to build more tailored and effective sales channel experiences for merchants and customers.

also_worth_knowing.text: Metafield triggers and additional topics are now available for Events
Liquid templates can now compose pages with blocks and partials
Full-stack capabilities to power app analytics

updates.title: Metafield triggers and additional topics are now available for Events
updates.summary: Metafield triggers and additional topics are now available for Events, allowing apps to subscribe to events for various objects and metafield changes.

updates.title: Liquid templates can now compose pages with blocks and partials
updates.summary: Liquid templates can now compose pages with blocks and partials, simplifying theme page building.

updates.title: Full-stack capabilities to power app analytics
updates.summary: Full-stack capabilities are now available to power app analytics, making it easier for app developers to build merchant-facing analytics.

updates.title: Physical inventory feature preview
updates.summary: The physical inventory feature preview is now available, providing early access to new inventory APIs in the unstable version of the GraphQL Admin API.
CLI version 4.5.2 fixes a warning when running theme commands in valid theme directories and bumps Shopify/theme-tools packages.

updates.title: Card deposit endpoint now requires mTLS certificate
updates.summary: The card deposit endpoint now requires a Shopify-issued mTLS client certificate, affecting apps that store cardholder data.

updates.title: @shopify/cli-hydrogen@13.0.2
updates.summary: Hydrogen CLI version 13.0.2 adds non-interactive shop and storefront creation flags and allows bypassing confirmation prompts in hydrogen upgrade.

updates.title: Updated App Store requirements: 4.1.2 Use a unique name for your app
updates.summary: Updated App Store requirements include using a unique name for your app to reduce merchant confusion.

updates.title: Storefront API `@inContext` supports `channelId`
updates.summary: The Storefront API @inContext directive now supports channelId, allowing for channel-specific context in queries.

updates.title: `productId`, `title`,`variantSku`, and `variantTitle` fields added to `ExchangeLineItem`
updates.summary: The ExchangeLineItem type now includes productId, title, variantSku, and variantTitle fields.

updates.title: POS UI Extensions 2026-07 adds discount allocations to bundle components
updates.summary: POS UI Extensions API version 2026-07 adds discount allocations to bundle components, allowing apps to access component-level discount allocations.

recap.headline: Enhanced Events, Liquid Templates, and App Analytics Capabilities

themes.title: Event Triggers and Metafields
themes.detail: Metafield triggers and additional topics are now available for Events, allowing apps to subscribe to events for various resources such as Order, Collection, and InventoryItem. Additionally, metafield changes can be subscribed to on Product, Order, Customer, Collection, and Location.

themes.title: Theme Development and App Analytics
themes.detail: Liquid templates can now compose pages with blocks and partials, simplifying theme page building. Furthermore, full-stack capabilities are now available to power app analytics, eliminating the need for a separate stack.

themes.title: Security and API Updates
themes.detail: The card deposit endpoint now requires a Shopify-issued mTLS client certificate, enhancing security. The Storefront API's @inContext directive now supports channelId, and new fields have been added to the ExchangeLineItem type.

actions.text: Update apps to use new event triggers and metafields
Explore the new Liquid template features
Implement mTLS client certificate for card deposit endpoint
Review and comply with updated App Store requirements
